breaking_news.title: FBI probes breach of internal system managing sensitive surveillance and wiretap data
breaking_news.summary:
The Federal Bureau of Investigation is actively investigating suspicious cyber activity affecting an internal network system that stores sensitive surveillance records and wiretap warrants. The incident, detected on February 17, 2026, involves abnormal log activity on a system containing law-enforcement sensitive data, including pen register and trap-and-trace orders, as well as personally identifiable information linked to ongoing investigations.

This breach poses a severe risk to national security and the integrity of federal law enforcement operations, as the compromised data could reveal active surveillance targets and investigative techniques. The FBI has notified members of Congress and is assessing the full scope of the intrusion, which raises concerns about potential state-sponsored actors targeting US intelligence infrastructure.

breaking_news.title: Iran-linked MuddyWater APT deploys new Dindoor backdoor against US banks and airports
breaking_news.summary:
The Iran-linked advanced persistent threat group MuddyWater has launched a campaign targeting US organizations across critical sectors, including banks, airports, and software companies. Researchers from Broadcom's Symantec Threat Hunter Team identified the deployment of a new backdoor named Dindoor, which has been active since February 2026 to infiltrate these high-value networks.

The compromise of financial and transportation infrastructure by a state-sponsored actor represents a significant escalation in cyber warfare capabilities and intent. The presence of MuddyWater in these networks suggests a strategic effort to gather intelligence or prepare for potential disruptive operations against US critical infrastructure during times of geopolitical tension.

breaking_news.title: Cisco warns of active exploitation of critical Catalyst SD-WAN vulnerabilities
breaking_news.summary:
Cisco has confirmed that threat actors are actively exploiting two recently patched vulnerabilities, CVE-2026-20128 and CVE-2026-20122, in its Catalyst SD-WAN Manager software. These flaws allow attackers to gain root access, overwrite arbitrary files, and elevate privileges on affected systems, prompting an urgent advisory for organizations to apply the latest security updates immediately.

The widespread exploitation of these SD-WAN flaws puts enterprise networks at severe risk of total compromise, as SD-WAN devices often serve as the primary edge for corporate connectivity. The rapid transition from patch release to active exploitation indicates a high level of sophistication and readiness among threat actors targeting network infrastructure.

breaking_news.title: Google identifies Coruna iOS exploit kit targeting spyware and crypto-theft campaigns
breaking_news.summary:
Google's Threat Intelligence Group has uncovered the Coruna exploit kit, a sophisticated toolchain containing 23 exploits across five chains targeting iOS versions 13.0 through 17.2.1. Originally developed for state-sponsored espionage, the kit has been repurposed by criminal groups for mass cyberespionage and cryptocurrency theft, marking a dangerous shift in the availability of high-end zero-day capabilities.

The proliferation of the Coruna kit highlights a growing secondary market for zero-day exploits, where nation-state tools are sold or leaked to criminal actors. This trend significantly increases the threat landscape for mobile users and enterprises relying on iOS devices, as the exploit kit can bypass standard security controls to install persistent spyware.

breaking_news.title: Massive GitHub malware operation spreads BoryptGrab stealer via 100+ repositories
breaking_news.summary:
Trend Micro discovered a large-scale campaign distributing the BoryptGrab information stealer through over 100 compromised GitHub repositories. The malware, disguised as legitimate software tools and game cheats, steals browser data, cryptocurrency wallet credentials, and system information, while some variants deploy a reverse SSH backdoor for persistent access.

This supply chain attack leverages the trust users place in open-source platforms to deliver sophisticated malware, posing a significant risk to developers and enterprises that rely on GitHub for code and tools. The scale of the operation suggests a coordinated effort to maximize data theft across a wide range of victims, including those in the financial and technology sectors.

breaking_news.title: LeakBase cybercrime forum seized in international operation disrupting 142,000 users
breaking_news.summary:
In a coordinated international operation led by Europol and the FBI, the LeakBase cybercrime forum was seized, disrupting a major marketplace for stolen data and hacking tools. The platform, which had grown to over 142,000 registered users, served as a central hub for trading leaked databases, credentials, and infostealer logs since 2021.

The takedown of LeakBase represents a significant blow to the global cybercrime ecosystem, removing a key infrastructure used to monetize data breaches. Law enforcement agencies in 14 countries executed search warrants and arrests, demonstrating a unified effort to dismantle the economic incentives driving large-scale data theft operations.

breaking_news.title: Microsoft warns of ClickFix campaign abusing Windows Terminal to deploy Lumma Stealer
breaking_news.summary:
Microsoft has identified a new ClickFix social engineering campaign that tricks users into launching Windows Terminal via the Windows + X shortcut to execute malicious PowerShell commands. This technique bypasses traditional Run dialog detections and security awareness training, ultimately deploying the Lumma Stealer malware to harvest credentials and sensitive data.

The evolution of ClickFix tactics to target trusted system utilities like Windows Terminal highlights the increasing sophistication of social engineering attacks. Organizations must update their endpoint detection strategies to monitor for unusual command-line activity within terminal emulators, as this vector is becoming a primary method for initial access.

breaking_news.title: Iran-linked hackers target IP cameras in Israel and Gulf states for military intelligence
breaking_news.summary:
Researchers observed a surge in attacks targeting IP cameras across Israel and Gulf countries, attributed to Iran-linked actors seeking to support military intelligence and battle damage assessment. The campaign exploits known vulnerabilities in Hikvision and Dahua Technology cameras to hijack devices and gather real-time visual intelligence during ongoing regional conflicts.

The weaponization of IoT infrastructure for military reconnaissance underscores the convergence of cyber warfare and physical conflict. This activity poses a direct threat to national security and critical infrastructure, as compromised cameras can be used to monitor troop movements, assess strike damage, and plan future military operations.
